purpose: Systematically compares what equities, credit, rates, and commodities each imply about the macro regime to identify tensions and mispricing
components: Commodity demand curve implied regime assessment
Verdict: rates closest to right, equities most likely to adjust, credit most dangerous because mispricing is model-embedded
current_assessment: Useful portfolio-level assessment tool. The identification of cross-asset tension is valuable; the specific verdict on which market is 'wrong' is lower confidence because markets price heterogeneous views from different participants with different horizons and mandates.
limitations: Markets routinely price heterogeneous views — tension does not necessarily imply mispricing
No single asset class can be identified as 'wrong' on demographic grounds alone
Tech concentration and AI earnings expectations may justify equity pricing independent of demographics
Credit spread supply-demand technicals (CLO bid, insurance yields) may justify spread levels
